title: BEM-924 curator response
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM924_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_RESPONSE.md with content # BEM-924 FINAL WORKING SYSTEM PROTOCOL RESPONSE
Status: APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS
Findings:
- Worker approves final working-system protocol with requirements.
- The protocol must synthesize the entire day and must not focus on Telegram, provider model or prompts alone.
- The final document must be an implementation contract: current state, gaps, target architecture, phased roadmap, acceptance criteria, proofs, operator gates and release definition.
- SSOT identity/provider/rule layer must be implemented before automation. Automation without identity/provider/rule SSOT repeats fake-autonomy.
- Definition of working system: operator submits task through registered channel; task enters GD curator route; policy/dispatch run; provider-aware contour lifecycle executes; proof with non-null release evidence is created; event log and managed channel update; canonical report returns; Claude re-audit verifies the result.
Evidence:
- governance/runtime/curator_dispatch/BEM924_AGENT_CONTEXT_READ.md
- governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM924_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_REQUEST.md
- governance/audit/internal/results/BEM924_WORKER_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md
Agreed roadmap:
- BEM-925 SSOT identity layer: objects-to-elements refs, elements_registry, contours_registry, providers_registry, provider policies, prompt templates and workflow concurrency baseline.
- BEM-926 rule and experience layer: dynamic rule refs, experience registry, inheritance validator, no local element rules by default.
- BEM-927 envelope and channel layer: managed channel consumer, telegram_input_envelope_schema, mapping, provider-aware envelope fields.
- BEM-928 dispatch lifecycle layer: queue state machine, auto-trigger design, provider-aware dispatch consumer, stale queue validator.
- BEM-929 object/contour execution layer: deterministic provider-aware contour runner with Analyst->Auditor->Executor->Auditor and failover events.
- BEM-930 proof and CI layer: non-null CI/Git SHA, release_proof_manifest, workflow concurrency and release gate.
- BEM-931 Telegram operator layer: existing webhook integration, GD curator main route, optional curator routes, report config owner OPERATOR and canonical reports.
- BEM-932 E2E validation layer: mock Telegram and production-status split, primary and reserve provider scenarios, managed channel, dispatch, runner and report proof.
- BEM-933 Claude re-audit and release package: fixed checklist before bundle, fact table, proof index and release decision.
Operator gates:
- real secrets/tokens, production webhook mutation, new schedule triggers, always-on daemon, issue #31 writes, paid API, LLM-agent runtime model and real pilot/client/scope/metrics.
Recommendation:
- Submit final protocol BEM-924 to operator for review.
No issue comments.
